title: Semgrep says GLM 5.2 beat Claude on one cyber benchmark — with caveats
friendlyTitle: A Chinese open model did well on a security test
summary: Semgrep tested models on an IDOR vulnerability-detection benchmark and found GLM 5.2 scoring ahead of Claude Code in its prompt-only setup, while Semgrep’s own purpose-built harness still led overall.
friendlySummary: Semgrep found that GLM 5.2 performed well at finding one kind of web app security bug, although Semgrep’s own specialised system still did better.
whyItMatters: The useful signal is not that one model has permanently won security work; it is that model choice and task harness both matter. Security teams should evaluate the full workflow around an AI model, including context selection, endpoint discovery, and output review.
friendlyWhyItMatters: The lesson is that the model is only one part of the tool. The setup around the model can be just as important.
